search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae palpitation telehealth clinical guideline PDF Dubai
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
The presentation does not establish a specific arrhythmia.
Treatment and dose content was not mapped.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for outpatients with palpitations, with no precise age range stated.
setting_applicability: Direct for DHA telehealth documentation; emergency presentations require escalation.
UAE_applicability: Direct for Dubai DHA-licensed telehealth; local monitoring, referral, and prescribing governance remains required.
recency_verification: DHA Issue 2 remains the current verified registry copy and is due for revision in 2029.
superseded_check: No newer official DHA issue was identified on the telehealth portal.

evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines DHA-licensed telehealth scope and emergency and controlled-medication exclusions.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped palpitation terms, symptoms, associated negatives, presets, and history.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies palpitation description, onset, duration, triggers, associated symptoms, substances, medical history, and examination context.
Lists chest pain, dizziness or syncope, breathlessness, focal neurology, dehydration, family sudden death, exertional onset, systemic causes, and hypotension.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped ECG, ambulatory monitoring, thyroid, blood-count, and electrolyte review.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists ECG, ambulatory monitoring, and blood tests selected from the clinical history.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped monitoring or referral and explicit referral documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists immediate and specialist referral criteria for palpitations.

unresolved_source_gaps: Generic examination prompts, broad clinician plans, medication plans, safety-netting, and generic follow-up remain unsupported.
Arrhythmia diagnosis, monitoring selection, referral, and treatment remain clinician-stated.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
